FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, N=11,875, ages 9-10 years, 21 recruitment sites

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical cross-sectional analysis; correlation and regression analyses reported

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 2 composite-content check: Youth Screen Time Survey composite includes (1) TV watching, (2) video watching, (3) video games, (4) texting, (5) social media, (6) video chat. Composite bundles TV and unspecified-platform video games with phone/SM items; multi-device aggregated without per-item breakout.

notes: Screen time composite includes TV, video games (platform unspecified), and video watching without separate per-item estimates or breakout.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD cohort substudy, N=67, ages 11-12 years, passive sensing pilot in 2019-2020

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; passive sensing protocol with descriptive statistics and correlation analyses

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article, JMIR Mental Health 2021

FT_IC2.evidence: Pure psychometric/validation paper. Smartphone screen use measured via passive sensing app; results are descriptive (means, SDs, percentages of app-category time) and validation-focused (correlations between self-report and objective data: r=0.49 for overall screen time; ANOVA of app use by demographics). No coefficient for association between SM variable and individual-trait outcome; SM appears as measured variable only.

notes: Feasibility/validation study of passive sensing methods; no outcome variable paired with SM variable.

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, N=11,875, ages 9-11 years, cross-sectional data from baseline (September 2016 - November 2018, release 2.0)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical cross-sectional analysis; negative binomial regression with prevalence ratios and 95% CIs reported

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article, Journal of Adolescent Health 2021

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 2 composite-content check: Youth Screen Time Survey measures (1) TV watching, (2) video watching (e.g., YouTube), (3) video game playing, (4) texting, (5) social media, (6) video chat. Daily recreational screen time calculated as weighted average of these six items. Composite bundles TV, unspecified-platform video games, and non-phone devices without separate per-item breakout or sensitivity analyses isolating phone/SM components.

notes: Screen time composite includes TV, video games (unspecified platform), and video watching; multi-device aggregated time without per-device breakout or isolation of phone/SM-only estimate.
